persons.arguments_against: TRENCH is common in this corpus (8 officials, 6 bios) and the bio gives only initials 'D. P.' with no birth year, so on its face this is a weak two-initial case. This fails because the stint supplies the FULL forename 'Daniel Power Trench', which uniquely expands the bio's D. P., and the office is a single high-level post: bio 'collector general of Jamaica upon the amalgamation of the customs, excise, and internal revenue departments in March, 1869' matches the staff list's 'Collector-General' (Revenue Department) verbatim. The bio's 'official member of the legislative council 1866' matches the Collector General appearing under Legislative Council / Official Members. Only one person can be Collector-General of Jamaica at a time; combined with the full-forename agreement, none of the other Trench bios could hold this stint.

evidence_cited.fact: collector general of Jamaica upon the amalgamation of the customs, excise, and internal revenue departments in March, 1869

persons.would_overturn: A second Daniel Power Trench, or evidence the staff-list Collector-General was a different D. P. Trench than the bio.

notes: Full forename 'Daniel Power' in the stint pins the bio's D. P. initials; the unique senior office Collector-General of Jamaica plus Legislative Council membership make this certain despite the common surname.
